learningObjectives: Name common sports and leisure activities using Starters vocabulary.
Use can to talk about what you are able to do.
Use play and go with sports and activities correctly.
Match activities to pictures in short texts.
Listen and identify sports, games and actions.
Talk about what you can do and what games you play.
grammarFocus: Can for ability: I can swim

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I can swim in the pool.
We play a game in the park.

grammarReference.structure: Can for ability: I can swim
grammarReference.explanation: Use can + verb to say what you are able to do. Can does not change with he, she, it.
commonMistakes: I can swims (×) → I can swim (✓)
He cans run (×) → He can run (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use play with ball games and games (play football, play tennis). Use go with -ing activities (go swimming). At Starters, play is most common.
commonMistakes: I go football (×) → I play football (✓)
play swim (×) → go swimming / can swim (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Sports and Games
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve sports and leisure words at Starters level.

exercises.title: Learn: Sports Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: Eleven players kick a _____ on a field.
questions.explanation: Football is played with a ball on a field. Tennis uses a racket; swim is an action in water.

choices.distractorNote: Another sport but not played with kicking on a field
Action in water, not a field sport

questions.questionText: You hit a small green object over a net with a racket. This sport is _____.
questions.explanation: Tennis is played with a racket and net. Football uses feet; bike is transport.

questions.questionText: In the pool, you move your arms and legs in the water. You _____.
questions.explanation: Swim means to move through water. Run is on land; play is general.

choices.distractorNote: Too general; does not name the water action

questions.questionText: You move fast on your feet. You _____.
questions.explanation: Run means to move quickly on foot. Jump goes up; kick hits with the foot.

choices.distractorNote: Up in the air, not forward fast
One foot action, not moving forward

questions.questionText: Children have fun with rules and take turns. They play a _____.
questions.explanation: A game is an activity with rules for fun. Ball is an object; sport is a category.

choices.distractorNote: Object used in games, not the activity itself
Broader category; game is more specific here

questions.questionText: You ride this with two wheels. It is a _____.
questions.explanation: A bike has two wheels and you ride it. Ball is round; game is an activity.

choices.distractorNote: Activity, not a thing with wheels

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the sports and action words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với một nghĩa rõ ràng. Kick và catch thường bị nhầm vì đều liên quan đến bóng.

pairs.right: hit the ball with your foot
stop the ball with your hands
a game like football or tennis

exercises.title: Check: Sports and Games Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.explanation: Ball (quả bóng) — đá trong bóng đá.
football/tennis cho môn; ball cho dụng cụ.

content.template: I play [0] with my friends. We use a [1].

exercises.title: Review: Sports and Games Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.explanation: Ball (quả bóng) — đá trong bóng đá.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Games, Ball and Bike
lessons.learningObjective: Use ball, game, bike, play and kick in sports contexts.

questions.explanation: Ghép hành động với dụng cụ.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: I Can Play!
lessons.learningObjective: Use can for ability and play with sports and games correctly.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose Can or Play
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: I _____ swim. I go to the pool every week.
questions.explanation: Can + swim nói về khả năng bơi. Play không đi với swim ở Starters.

choices.distractorNote: Play goes with games/sports, not swim

questions.explanation: Play tennis là cụm đúng. Can tennis không đúng ngữ pháp.

choices.distractorNote: Can needs a verb: can play tennis
Go tennis is incorrect; go swimming is the pattern

questions.explanation: Can + catch hỏi khả năng bắt bóng. Can ball thiếu động từ.

choices.distractorNote: Noun, not a verb after can

questions.explanation: Play football là cụm chuẩn. Go football sai; can football thiếu động từ.

exercises.title: Practice: Can and Play
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with can, play or run.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] swim. | We [1] football. | He can [2] fast.
questions.explanation: Can swim (khả năng); play football (môn thể thao); run fast (can + động từ).

content.template: I [0] swim. We [1] football. He can [2] fast.

questions.explanation: Can + động từ nguyên mẫu — khả năng.
We play — số nhiều không có -s.
Can + động từ; play + môn thể thao.

content.template: She [0] play tennis. I [1] football every day.

questions.explanation: Can + động từ nguyên mẫu — khả năng.
We play — số nhiều không có -s.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Can for Ability
lessons.learningObjective: Use can and can't to talk about what you are able to do.

questions.questionText: She _____ swim — she is afraid of water.
questions.explanation: Can't = cannot — phủ định khả năng.

content.template: Tom [0] run fast. My sister [1] swim.

questions.explanation: Can you + động từ? — hỏi khả năng.

choices.distractorNote: Do you play — cũng được nhưng Can hỏi ability trực tiếp

questions.explanation: He can jump — ngôi thứ ba vẫn dùng can.
Can you + động từ? — hỏi khả năng.

choices.distractorNote: Do you play — cũng được nhưng Can hỏi ability trực tiếp

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Play and Go
lessons.learningObjective: Use play with sports and go with activities correctly.

content.template: I [0] tennis. We [1] swimming in the pool.

questions.explanation: She plays — ngôi thứ ba có -s.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Sports Day
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short text about sports and match activities to descriptions.

exercises.title: Learn: Read: Sports Day at School
exercises.instructions: Read the text. Then answer the questions.

passage.title: Sports Day at School
passage.text:
Hello! My name is Ben. Today is Sports Day at school.

I can run fast. My friend Kim can jump high. Tom plays football. He can kick the ball very well.

After lunch, we play a game. Anna can catch the ball. Lily plays tennis. She has a racket and a ball.

In the afternoon, we ride our bikes. Sports Day is fun!
passage.imagePrompt: A school sports day: children running, kicking a football, playing tennis, and riding bikes on a field.

questions.questionText: What is the main idea of the text?
questions.explanation: Toàn bộ văn bản mô tả các hoạt động thể thao trong ngày hội thể thao ở trường.

choices.text: Ben and friends do sports and games at school.
Ben talks about his favourite food.
Ben describes his house and street.

questions.explanation: Văn bản nói: My friend Kim can jump high.

questions.questionText: Why does Tom kick the ball?
questions.explanation: Tom plays football — trong bóng đá người ta đá bóng.

choices.text: Because he is riding a bike.
choices.distractorNote: Bikes come later in the text

questions.questionText: In 'She has a racket and a ball', what sport does Lily play?
questions.explanation: Racket và ball gợi ý tennis — câu trước cũng nói Lily plays tennis.

choices.distractorNote: Football uses feet, not a racket

questions.questionText: Put the ideas in order: (1) Ben tells what he and friends can do (2) They play a game at lunch (3) They ride bikes in the afternoon
questions.explanation: Văn bản đi theo thứ tự: sáng (run, jump, football) → trưa (game) → chiều (bikes).

choices.distractorNote: Morning activities come before lunch game

exercises.title: Practice: Find Sports in the Text

passage.title: Sports Day
passage.text:
Today is Sports Day at school. We run and play games.

Tom can run fast. Anna can swim well. I can play football.

My friend likes tennis. We use a ball for many games.

Look! Ben rides his bike. Sports are fun!
passage.imagePrompt: School sports day with running, football, tennis and bike.

questions.questionText: What sport uses a ball for many games?
questions.explanation: We use a ball for many games.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Weekend and After School
lessons.learningObjective: Understand when and where leisure activities happen in texts.

passage.title: Weekend Fun
passage.text:
On Saturday I play football in the park with my friends.

On Sunday we swim in the pool. My sister can't swim — she plays with a ball.

Father runs every morning. Can you ride a bike? Yes, I can!

We love sports and games!
passage.imagePrompt: Park and pool scenes with football, swimming, running and bike.

questions.explanation: On Saturday I play football in the park.

passage.title: After School
passage.text:
School finishes at three o'clock. What do you do after school?

I play a game on the computer. Then I go outside.

My brother plays tennis. I ride my bike. We run in the garden.

In the evening we are tired. Sport makes us strong!
passage.imagePrompt: Children playing football, tennis and riding bikes after school.

script.title: At the Playground

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Hello, Sam. What can you do?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Great! What sport do you play?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: I play football. I can kick the ball.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Yes. She can swim and jump.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Do you play a game now?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Yes. We play a ball game.

script.audioNotes: Clear, slow delivery with short pauses. Total duration approx. 38 seconds.

questions.explanation: Sam nói: I can run fast!
Sam nói chị/em gái can swim and jump.

lines.text: Yes! He can run fast too.
